event_title_eval: GET PHIT Bootcamp - Prairie View A&M University (PVAMU) May 2025
course_changes: The two-week program offered invaluable opportunities that I believe every student should have the chance to experience. I learned new skills and gained insights that are rarely taught in the traditional classroom setting. I would recommend expanding access to this program so that more students across diverse backgrounds and disciplines can benefit from its unique blend of hands-on learning, mentorship, and career exposure.
general_comments: I recommend allowing students access to Canvas for at least one to two days after the program officially ends. We were informed that Canvas would remain available through the weekend to download materials; however, many of us were logged out earlier than expected. Extending access would ensure participants have adequate time to retrieve valuable resources, review content, and reinforce what they‚Äôve learned.
supportive_factors: All instructors demonstrated strong communication skills, offering clear direction and support throughout the course. Their approachable and encouraging style made it easier to grasp complex concepts and stay engaged. The instructors consistently prioritized our safety and created a supportive environment where students felt secure and respected, both physically and emotionally. By instilling confidence in our abilities and exposing us to future academic and career pathways, the instructors empowered us to see our potential beyond the program. This was an invaluable part of the learning experience.
independent_analysis_level: Yes; I am capable of performing analysis independently.

event_title_eval: GET PHIT Bootcamp - University of Texas at Arlington (UTA) June 2025
improvement_suggestions: Implement activities during the lecture week. It wasnt easy to sit through hours of lecture at a time. Additionally, the material was very public health-focused rather than data and informatics-focused. When the program was advertised to me, it gave the impression that it would be very tech-integrated, but we were only instructed on how to use Excel. Some lectures on Tableau and ArcGIS, as well as hands-on experience with code, would have been great skills to learn.
course_changes: Including more lectures about coding and hands-on experience in using software to facilitate data analysis. We only really got a lecture covering Excel during our first week. More time spent in ArcGIS or even Tableau would have been better, since most of us already use Excel.
general_comments: Overall, coming from a Public health student, the information taught at the bootcamp was vital in understanding PHI, but was more skewed to understanding public health. Lots of the information was review for me. I believe it needs to be equivalent to the amount of tech information taught. So, more tech and data-driven lectures as opposed to mostly public health information. Additionally, including activities in each lesson, since there was such a time constraint, maybe cutting down on the lecture materials to leave enough room for activity work. Lots of people learn best when applying the info, which aids students in retaining information they have just learned. I was hoping to get hands-on experience in coding or other software besides Excel, but given the time constraint, I guess that part couldnt able to be taught. I really was appreciative of the internship opportunities post bootcamp, I believe this helps equip students in getting their foot in the door post graduation!
supportive_factors: During our project week, our instructors were readily available to us when needed. When structuring our presentation, they aided us in what parts were the most important to include. Additionally, they helped us look for data in places we didnt have access to. Which was extremely helpful.
interest_topics_1: Decision Modeling and Natural Language Processing.
independent_analysis_level: No; I would require a significant amount of assistance.

event_title_eval: GET PHIT Bootcamp - University of Texas at El Paso (UTEP) July 2025
course_changes: I would recommend placing a stronger focus on hands-on learning with public health data systems and analytic tools that are actually used in the field. There should be step-by-step instruction from instructors on how to compile, clean, and analyze data using programs like R or SAS, rather than relying on peer presentations. Reducing the number of peer-to-peer presentations and increasing structured, skills-based training would make the program much more valuable.
general_comments: While some instructors were helpful, there was at least one mentor who created a discouraging environment by making students feel unintelligent for not understanding things right away. This made it difficult to feel comfortable asking questions or fully engaging. I hope that future versions of this program focus more on instructor quality, hands on technical skills, and creating a supportive environment where students can confidently learn and grow.
supportive_factors: Dr. Ross and Dr. Wagler were both kind and approachable, which helped create a more welcoming environment. The final project gave us an opportunity to work as a team and apply some data skills, even if it wasn‚Äôt as structured as I had hoped.
independent_analysis_level: No; I would require a significant amount of assistance.

event_title_eval: GET PHIT Bootcamp - University of Texas at El Paso (UTEP) July 2025
improvement_suggestions: I had a great deal of trouble navigating site, but material and content was very informative. We learned a great deal on the importance of interactions between different fields and how they all come together in public health. I believe taking a small amount of time from each class and having us the create a basic document in use of Exell, would be helpful. We were taught the different programs to create document, but I think the practical part in creating an actual document would help.
course_changes: Have a small part of IT based program hands on to create a sample of each program.
disagreement_reason_1: I learned another way to get correct citations that are very accurate from another group member. We were able to see each others strengths and weaknesses. The importance of good strong communications skills for team work.
least_fav_other: group work was challenging, it needs specific amounts of equally distributed work for it to be a group project. If only one person is judging what may or may not be included, it is not equal in contribution. I would say assigning maybe intro to one student and methods to another, discussion, etc,,etc would ensure all group members are allowed to voice there ideas into presentation. Sorry, but I must answer honestly as to what I perceive. There is no disrespect intended to any one person or instructor. If an instructor shows preferential treatment, when answering questions, to one particular student, then the project is not equal. We all have differnt levels of understanding and learning, although it is easier to teach a student with more understanding than one without, because of time constraints. It leaves the other 4 group members without a voice or input. We cannot let one person shine and not give all group members equity. If you are willing to teach one person a specific item to create, all members should also be taught the equally together, the same item at the same time, so it doesnt look like preference is being shown. You cant answer one person and silence another student.
general_comments: I absoluely loved it
supportive_factors: availability f information patience in teaching instructor williness to help

event_title_eval: GET PHIT Bootcamp - University of Texas Permian Basin (UTPB) July 2025
improvement_suggestions: The reason I picked disagree to the instructional materials question was for a couple of reasons. First, the presentation materials were generally not the most well explained and depending on the lecturer could create issues with understanding. Additionally, in a couple of the lectures the content was not well explained, especially to an audience who might be unfamiliar with the material, such as with simple linear regression. Though when there was a lecturer who was good an explaining and giving examples to the material, I feel this bootcamp was at its strongest.
course_changes: I would like the integration of current research on various topics covered in this course. Not even necessarily covering the research as a class, just being shown some of the reputable and important journals in fields related to public health informatics.
general_comments: I believe that some of the lectures and materials for them could be better suited to the average undergraduate who may not be the most familiar with statistics or at the very least could be oriented to basic statistics that can be explained thoroughly in a couple of hours, such as T-tests or Chi-square, rather than something like regression. I found the explanations of regression to be lacking in clarity and practical applications to our projects and generally felt like the time could have been better spent. Overall though, this bootcamp was an amazing experience that I am very happy to have had the opportunity to attend. So thank you for going through the process of creating this program and bringing it to UTPB.
supportive_factors: Dr. Ross was incredible at creating an engaged learning environment through mini-presentations and class discussions. I feel this helped going into the 2nd week as we already had to work together some with our group and knew some weaknesses and strengths.
independent_analysis_level: Yes; I am capable of performing analysis independently.

event_title_eval: GET PHIT Bootcamp - University of Texas Permian Basin May 2024
improvement_suggestions: The provided datasets for the project were not exactly helpful, some pacing and PowerPoints for the course did not seem necessary. I believe the program would benefit from being more pointed/specific in its goals and direction. Learning about epidemiology for two hours was not useful for our end project.
course_changes: The group system needs to be reworked. There were issues among groups that could have been solved with more attentive or robust management, or if people were allowed to choose groups after the first or second day of the camp. Additionally, the speed of the course feels a bit uneasy. The first week seemed to fade away and not necessarily prepare us for research if we did not already understand what to do. We spent hours on figuring out thirty second introduction speeches - that is not necessary.
disagreement_reason_1: The objectives and learning outcomes of this Bootcamp were relatively non-precise. I feel that too many topics were presented in a disorganized way that did not help me understand what the end learning goal was, that was something that I have gleaned myself. I genuinely do not feel that I have gained any new work skills from this Bootcamp, though this may be because I entered with a background in this already.
disagreement_reason_2: Ethnicity and culture regions were heavily dependent on who is presenting these items. I think they were good, but must be more pointed to talk about racial/ethnicity bias.
general_comments: I enjoyed it, but this could definitely be done better and there are ways that I and other students could be involved in planning to ensure that it is more efficient, successful, and productive in learning outcomes.
supportive_factors: Instructors were open to questions and open to helping students understand. The course is definitely important for many fields to understand. Allowing for a multitude of backgrounds to participate encourages student engagement.
interest_topics_1: Decision Modeling and Natural Language Processing.
independent_analysis_level: No; I would require a little assistance.

improvement_suggestions: The lecturing resources and materials were great in helping us understand why researching and public health are important. The instructors were great at helping us keep our research information organized and they were extremely supportive. It would also be good to have more breaks in between lectures since just having one every four hours was a bit taxing to our bodies.
course_changes: I would recommend to promote the boot camp more since the only reason I learned about it was because my professor sent an email about it. instructor_comments: I noticed that Dr. Cervantes edited the slides of his part of the presentation & even added a few of his own to further explain & help us understand certain concepts. improvement_suggestions: I feel that using other methods besides lecturing to teach us the information of the course would make this boot camp a lot better. To have us sitting in one room for 6+ hours listening to instructors just talk & reread things we can see on the slides doesn‚Äôt really instill information in anyone.
course_changes: I recommend presenting the criteria for the final project first before starting any of the lectures, that way we as students know which topics are going to be most relevant to us & the research topics we choose.
general_comments: I really did not appreciate the fact that the dorm facilities were extremely dirty upon arrival, & that the a/c in the dorms was not working at ALL. As someone who‚Äôs been going to HT this is nothing new for me, but to have to explain to someone who doesn‚Äôt attend this university, that it‚Äôs always like that is actually quite embarrassing . It would just be better for the school over all to make sure their facilities are working before inviting anyone to come & stay. It‚Äôs a joke to have to have stayed there for two weeks with no a/c. I realize that those who were running the bootcamp have no say in whether the a/c works but still. Guess it‚Äôs just something to look forward to in the school year.
supportive_factors: Like I mentioned before, Dr. Cervantes adding his own touch to the slides was really neat. Allowing our groups to work separately away from the other groups is another I liked about the project. For my team I know this helped us focus a lot more rather than being in the same room as everyone else & there being more distractions. Lastly, I enjoyed the portion of the camp where Dr.Ross was speaking to us about our future careers. She encouraged us to really look at the requirements needed to get into the schools & programs we wanted, & this was really an eye opener for me.

Providing food or figuring out what can be done in order for students to have access to meals, as the PV faculty members were not aware that we had not received money for food, and was extremely kind enough to get us breakfast and lunch every day out of their own pockets. And finally, having a better communication with the school that you are partnering with, since the instructors/faculty members were not aware of a lot of things and situations until we brought it to their attention.
least_fav_other: The process of registering for payroll, as we felt very neglected by UTH, due to that fact that we were told two hours before the deadline for direct deposit. And even after then, we had to call Helpdesk to assist us with many issues that we were running into. They would then send us to HR who then sent us back to them, etc. We were receiving different information and instructions from UTH staff members, all while the PVAMU faculty was not even aware of what was going on.
most_fav_other: I also really enjoyed the faculties lectures and overall interactions, along with having the privilege of creating new connections and friendships with other students in the Bootcamp.
general_comments: The Bootcamp was good overall and I am extremely happy that it took place at PV because the faculty members were great. However, I do believe that certain changes must be made on UTHs end to further improve the quality of the Bootcamp, along with everything that falls under it.
supportive_factors: The instructors were kind patient and considerate of each student, the course was very interesting, and the event and activities promoted interaction between students present. interest_topics_1: Health Care Management and Technology Adoption.

event_title_eval: GET PHIT Bootcamp - Texas A&M International University (TAMIU) July 2024
improvement_suggestions: Actually going in depth in how to use excel.
course_changes: I actually really hope this gets read. I think that in order to accomplish the GET aspect to GET-PHIT - we need to focus more on actually helping the students develop the skills regarding interpreting and analyzing data using tools like excel and IBM SPSS. It would have been better that more time was dedicated to making sure we had these skills and THEN asking groups to develop an idea using the data of what they would like to develop as a project. This way students with good ideas and continued interest in learning and presenting have until September to get together with their mentors throughout the two month span and develop their projects into something with potential to make change. Two weeks does not feel enough to truly delve into literature and find relevant sources. This course has the potential to change lives and introduce people into a growing field - the time is what is missing, but I still feel that I did NOT gain equity in training and I am saddened by this as I was truly interested and STILL AM interested in the field of public health. I, as an underprivileged and impoverished first gen jewish queer woman of colour, cannot say I walked away with skills that put me on par with people who are socioeconomically better off than me - and who actually dominate the field such as white folk, men, and cishet well off people.
disagreement_reason_1: I feel that there was not enough time dedicated to learning how to read and analyze the data sets we were looking at. I would have really liked to have learned how to use the tool pack to analyze the data more in depth.
general_comments: Id appreciate it a lot if feedback like mine was listened to. This program was incredible and continues to have a lot of potential. Hoping to attend next year if possible.
supportive_factors: The class days that discussed how different fields have a place in public health and the different fields we could enter were exciting and helpful to learn about. Dr.Fenton was also very approachable and kind and open to provide insight and personal advise for those wanting to enter the field.
interest_topics_1: Health Care Management and Technology Adoption.
interest_topics_2: Surveillance and how to develop better EHR recording and tracking to reduce disparities in accumulated data regarding BIPOC and LGBTQ people.
independent_analysis_level: No; I would require a significant amount of assistance.
